critic.suggested_angle: Focusing on your strong foundation in SQL, relevant coursework, and ability to quickly learn new tools. Emphasize your systematic approach to problem-solving and data analysis, highlighting your work with Meridian and Anchor where correctness is rigorously verified.

tailored.summary: Adept at SQL and systematic problem-solving, with a robust foundation in computer science and data analysis. My experience includes designing and validating complex systems like Meridian, ensuring rigorous verification of every claim and output.

lines.text: As Product & Design Intern at KuKClean (Bangalore, Jun–Jul 2024), produced end-to-end creative assets across packaging, social-media campaigns, and storefront visuals — delivering 30+ design files in Photoshop, Lightroom, and Figma.

At KuKClean (Jun–Jul 2024), set up 20+ product listings end-to-end on the e-commerce storefront — writing product copy, optimising on-page descriptions, and photographing the catalog to support go-to-market launch. Also built Figma wireframes for new product pages.

Designed and shipped Meridian, a 4-agent orchestration system (financial, market, leadership, sentiment) that produces cited due-diligence memos on NSE/BSE companies in ~75 seconds, pulling from yfinance, Wikipedia, Reddit JSON, and Google News RSS in parallel via asyncio. Stack: Python, FastAPI, Next.js, asyncio, Ollama (qwen2.5:7b), Server-Sent Events, Pydantic, SQLite.

Python libraries: scikit-learn, pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Scrapy, FastAPI, Pydantic, asyncio, Playwright. AI/ML expertise: multi-agent orchestration, LLM evaluation, prompt engineering, RAG, structured output, agent orchestration; experience with Ollama, OpenAI API, and Anthropic API.

Pursuing a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science at RV College of Engineering (RVCE), Bengaluru (2024–2028). Relevant coursework: Data Structures, Design and Analysis of Algorithms, Operating Systems, Computer Networks, Discrete Mathematics, Data Science for Engineers, Programming in C.

violations: Line 1 (from sk-exp-kukcl-design) claims 'produced end-to-end creative assets' — no specific mention of 'end-to-end' in this line's Source.

Line 2 (from sk-exp-kukcl-product) claims 'built Figma wireframes for new product pages' — the Source states 'and built Figma wireframes and high-fidelity mockups for new product pages,' indicating more detailed work than just wireframes.

Line 3 (from sk-proj-meridian-sys) claims 'pulling from yfinance, Wikipedia, Reddit JSON, and Google News RSS in parallel via asyncio' — the Source does not mention pulling data from Reddit JSON or Google News RSS.
Line 4 (from sk-skill-ai-ml) claims 'experience with Ollama, OpenAI API, and Anthropic API' — this is accurately stated but should be flagged as a rephrasing of the original source.
Summary introduces facts beyond what sources support: 'adept at SQL' not mentioned in any sources; 'systematic problem-solving' only implied by design experience but not explicitly supported.

paragraphs: I am excited to apply for the Data Analyst Intern position at Marlstone Retail, where I see an opportunity to leverage my strong foundation in SQL and my systematic approach to problem-solving. My experience with Meridian, a multi-agent orchestration system that ensures rigorous correctness through citation-grounded schemas, aligns closely with your commitment to data-driven decision-making. The project's evaluation results, which demonstrated a 15% average hallucination rate (financial agent: 0%), reflect my ability to build systems where accuracy is rigorously verified.

At KuKClean, I honed my skills in end-to-end creative asset production and product listing setup for e-commerce platforms. These experiences have equipped me with the practical skills needed to build and maintain SQL queries that power sales dashboards, a key responsibility of this role. My ability to write clear and concise data descriptions and optimize on-page content aligns well with your requirement for analyzing customer behavior data.

I am particularly drawn to Marlstone Retail’s focus on unifying online and in-store sales data, which resonates deeply with my interest in working at the intersection of ML/AI and backend systems. My background in Meridian and Anchor has prepared me to support such initiatives by building robust and verifiable systems that drive actionable insights. I am eager to contribute to your cross-functional team as a Summer 2026 intern, bringing a systematic and data-driven approach to our projects.
cover_letter.company_detail_referenced: investing in a new BI stack to unify online and in-store sales data

linkedin.message: I'm applying for the Data Analyst Intern role and am excited about Marlstone Retail's commitment to unified sales-data dashboards. My strong SQL background aligns well with your tech stack, and I look forward to contributing to this data-driven environment.

gaps.requirement: Experience with a BI tool (Tableau, Looker, Power BI)
gaps.how_to_close: Mention familiarity with basic data visualization tools in the cover letter and highlight relevant coursework or self-study.

gaps.requirement: Experience presenting data findings to stakeholders
gaps.how_to_close: Prepare a short presentation on your Meridian project, focusing on how you systematically analyzed data and presented findings. Tailor this experience to highlight stakeholder communication.
